title: Sausage Rolls

overview.description: Crisp all-butter puff pastry wrapped around well-seasoned pork sausage meat and baked until golden.

ingredients: 500g good-quality pork sausage meat (or skins removed from quality sausages)
method: Mix the sausage meat with the sage, thyme, pepper and Dijon mustard if using.
Unroll the pastry and cut it lengthways into two equal strips.
Shape the sausage meat into two even logs and place one along the centre of each strip.
Brush one edge of each pastry strip with beaten egg.
Fold the pastry over the filling and press firmly to seal.
Turn each roll so the seam sits underneath.
Brush generously with beaten egg and sprinkle with seeds if using.
Cut a few small slashes in the top of each roll.
Bake for 25–30 minutes until deep golden and the filling reaches 75°C.
Cool on a wire rack for at least 10 minutes before serving.

learning.did-you-know: Sausage rolls have been popular in Britain since the early 1800s, becoming a staple of bakeries and picnic hampers.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to shape evenly filled sausage rolls, seal pastry correctly and bake it until crisp and flaky.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: Perfect for lunchboxes, picnics, packed lunches and freezer meals. They freeze beautifully and are far better than most shop-bought versions.
chapman-tips: Use the best sausages you can afford—the filling is the star.
Keep the puff pastry cold until you're ready to use it.
Don't overfill or the pastry may split during baking.
Allow the rolls to cool slightly before eating so the filling firms up.
notes.batch-cooking: Freeze unbaked or baked sausage rolls for up to 3 months.
notes.leftovers: Excellent cold in lunchboxes or reheated in the oven to restore the crisp pastry.
